hero.title: Digital Twin Agentic Solution for Emergency Management
hero.description: Build a live digital twin for regions, cities, parks, and critical sites, then layer LLM-based assessment and multi-agent execution across monitoring, decision support, plan simulation, resource dispatch, field coordination, and post-incident review.
hero.imageAlt: Emergency command and dispatch visualization screen

intelligence.title: From seeing an event to organizing a response
items: Sensor, video, IoT, and business data are registered in one twin base to form a live operating picture.
The LLM turns fragmented event information into risk summaries, response suggestions, and command wording.
Agents keep tracking teams, supplies, notices, tasks, and review actions so instructions become execution.

architecture.title: Three-layer capability architecture
architecture.description: This is not just a command screen. It separates situation awareness, model reasoning, and execution coordination into deployable layers that can grow from one pilot scenario to a city-scale emergency system.

items.title: Digital twin situation base
items.description: Map cities, parks, watersheds, roads, critical facilities, and risk sources into a virtual-real model for event localization, impact analysis, and resource reachability.
points: Unified spatial map and risk objects
Live overlay of events, teams, supplies, and facilities
Simulation for flooding, fire, congestion, evacuation, and more

items.title: LLM assessment center
items.description: Support natural-language queries, event summaries, plan retrieval, risk explanation, and communication drafting for command teams.
points: Retrieval of plans and historical cases
Traceable assessment evidence and action suggestions

items.title: Multi-agent execution
items.description: Divide monitoring, assessment, dispatch, notification, public communication, and review into cooperating agents that keep advancing the event workflow.
points: Status tracking for teams, vehicles, supplies, and arrival
Incident timeline, outcome review, and optimization suggestions

flow.title: Cover the full emergency response lifecycle
flow.description: Create a loop across pre-event warning, in-event command, and post-event review, turning every response into better capability for the next one.

steps.title: Risk monitoring
steps.description: Connect weather, water, fire, traffic, video, and IoT data to detect and locate abnormal events earlier.

steps.title: Situation twin
steps.description: Overlay people, facilities, risk sources, shelters, warehouses, and event progress on one twin map.

steps.title: Intelligent assessment
steps.description: Read structured and unstructured information to generate incident level, impact area, secondary risks, and response priorities.

steps.title: Plan simulation
steps.description: Compare response plans and dispatch strategies against event level, roads, resources, weather, and safety constraints.

steps.title: Command coordination
steps.description: Agents track tasks, teams, supplies, notifications, and acknowledgements to keep the command center synchronized.

steps.title: Review optimization
steps.description: Compile timelines, bottlenecks, resource consumption, and outcome reviews to improve plans and the knowledge base.

agents.title: Agent matrix for emergency management
agents.description: Each agent owns a clear emergency task domain and collaborates through shared situation, shared knowledge, and shared task state.

items.title: Monitoring and warning agent
items.description: Analyzes indicators, thresholds, video clues, and public reports to identify anomalies and trigger graded warnings.

items.title: Situation briefing agent
items.description: Turns event progress, department feedback, field photos, and historical records into readable command briefings.

items.title: Plan simulation agent
items.description: Retrieves plans based on event type and constraints, then creates response paths and risk notes.

items.title: Resource dispatch agent
items.description: Matches teams, vehicles, supplies, equipment, and experts, calculates arrival time, and tracks allocation status.

items.title: Field coordination agent
items.description: Pushes tasks, routes, safety notes, and acknowledgement requirements to field teams with less manual relay.

items.title: Review assessment agent
items.description: Generates review reports, key timelines, response metrics, and plan revision suggestions.

scenarios.title: Start with four emergency scenarios
scenarios.description: Begin with frequent, high-impact scenarios that clearly require cross-agency coordination, then turn them into demonstrable and repeatable industry templates.

items.title: Extreme weather and urban flooding
items.description: Combine rainfall, water levels, roads, pump stations, and shelters to simulate flooding, detours, and relocation strategies.

items.title: Hazardous chemicals and industrial parks
items.description: Assess impact areas and response boundaries using enterprise data, hazard sources, wind direction, population, and fire resources.

items.title: Forest and urban fire coordination
items.description: Fuse fire points, wind field, combustible materials, fire stations, and water sources to support spread simulation and force deployment.

items.title: Large events and public safety
items.description: Monitor crowds, traffic, security positions, and medical support to detect congestion and conflict risks earlier.

practice.title: Build the solution from existing practice summaries
practice.description: The page abstracts existing case summaries into an industry solution, preserving emergency command, intelligent decision, resource dispatch, and visualization strengths while adding digital twin simulation and agentic coordination.
practice.imageTitle: Upgrade platform capabilities into a closed-loop emergency solution

items.label: Practice 01
items.title: Provincial Emergency Department · Emergency Command Platform
items.description: Reuse the AI command decision support capability from the case, strengthening incident analysis, prediction, assessment, plan management, resource dispatch, and visualization.

items.label: Practice 02
items.title: Digital twin simulation capability transfer
items.description: Apply modeling, simulation, and optimization patterns from digital twin platforms to disaster spread, evacuation paths, resource arrival, and secondary risk simulation.

items.label: Practice 03
items.title: Public safety multi-agency intelligence fusion
items.description: Use multi-department data fusion experience from public safety cases to strengthen city-scale risk identification and coordinated response.

cta.title: Start with one emergency scenario template
cta.description: Choose flooding, hazardous chemicals, fire response, or large-event safety first. Build data access, twin situation, assessment Q&A, plan simulation, and dispatch loop, then expand to a regional emergency management system.
